escalation_history.reason: Stage-1 Opus review (base:code-reviewer) found 4 issues at the untrusted-wire validation chokepoint: (sev5) updatedAt/since not ISO-8601-validated, enabling an LWW-poisoning attack via a lexicographically-dominant non-date string; (sev2) ParseResult's failure reason was an open string instead of a closed union; (sev2) no length bound on nickname/avatar.imageUrl on the untrusted parse path; (sev3) no imageUrl scheme allow-list.

retrospective.harder_than_needed: The initial implementation followed pairingAck.ts's validation template closely but under-scoped 'validate the wire shape' to structural/type checks only, missing that a field feeding a downstream LWW string-compare (AC-PROF-10) needs semantic validation (genuinely-parseable, uniformly-formatted ISO-8601), not just typeof-string. The gate-remediation review caught this before it reached story 04; a pre-impl verification question specifically about the updatedAt/since semantic contract (beyond 'is it a string') would have surfaced this during the original design pass instead of after.
retrospective.surprised_by: Checking the existing precedent for the imageUrl-scheme question (per the review's own instruction to check before adding new validation) turned up that the pre-existing MLS group profile-sync path (marmot/profileSync.ts#payloadToMemberProfile) and its render sites (ProfileSummary.tsx, AvatarBrowserModal.tsx) perform NO scheme validation on wire-sourced imageUrl today — this codec is being hardened past an existing, unfixed gap in an adjacent, already-shipped feature. Recorded as VQ-01-018 rather than fixed here (out of this story's scope); worth a follow-up finding against profileSync.ts if the lead wants that class of gap closed project-wide.

absorbed_from.note: Stage-1 Opus review: 4 findings (sev5 updatedAt/since ISO validation gap enabling LWW poisoning; sev2 open-string ParseResult reason; sev2 unbounded nickname/imageUrl length on untrusted parse path; sev3 no imageUrl scheme allow-list). All 4 applied; see verification.json VQ-01-015..VQ-01-018 for the post-impl analysis of each.
